page: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a virtual machine in the DCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1897.68 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1660.0. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively weak. The stress-ng multi-core score is 3765.27, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 2998.0. Uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is measured at 27.83 GB/sec, and write bandwidth is 19.41 GB/sec. Single-threaded operations like gzip compression at 28.57 GB/sec and small-model LLM inference at 420.8 tokens/sec for prompt processing perform at average levels, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor at 19.65 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the DC2s_v3 offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without the overhead of larger instances. The primary tradeoff is the limited processing capability of its dual-core configuration, which restricts its utility for highly parallelized tasks, database operations, or intensive web serving. It is best suited for lightweight memory-bound applications, single-threaded processing tasks, basic text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of memory, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, but includes no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average single-core execution, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1660.0 and average small-model LLM inference speeds. However, multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 2998.0 and a large-block memory read speed of 27.83 GB/sec. This instance represents a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight memory-bound applications, single-threaded data processing, and small-scale text processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a dedicated 2-vCPU Intel Xeon virtual machine with 16 GB RAM, offering average single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a 2-vCPU Intel Xeon virtual machine with 16 GB RAM, suitable for lightweight memory-bound workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core Intel processor instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight, memory-bound workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
